title: Day 10 Interview Prep
description: Practice keys, list rendering, filtering, and React.memo performance basics.

questions.question: Why do React lists need keys?
keyPoints: Help React identify which items changed
Not passed to component as prop
questions.sampleAnswer: Keys help React match list items across re-renders during reconciliation. When the list reorders or filters, stable keys tell React which DOM nodes correspond to which data. Without keys, React may reuse wrong component instances — losing state and causing bugs.

questions.question: Why should you avoid array index as key?
keyPoints: Acceptable only for static never-reordered lists
questions.sampleAnswer: When a list is sorted or filtered, index no longer maps to the same item. React reuses component instances at each index — wrong product data or lost input focus. I use product.id unless the list is static and never reordered.

questions.question: What is React.memo and when do you use it?
questions.sampleAnswer: React.memo wraps a component and skips re-render if props are shallowly equal to last render. I use it on ProductCard in large grids where parent filter state changes but most product props stay the same. I profile first — memo is not free.

questions.question: How do you implement category, price, search, and sort filters together?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Array chaining in real UI code.
keyPoints: Chain filter methods or single filter with compound condition
questions.sampleAnswer: I keep category, minPrice, maxPrice, search, and sortBy in state. useMemo derives filteredProducts: filter by category, filter by price range, filter by search term, then sort a copied array. Components render sortedProducts.map with product.id keys.

questions.question: How do you model a Product in TypeScript for an e-commerce listing?
questions.sampleAnswer: Product interface with id: number, title: string, price: number, category: string, image: string, optional description. SortOption as union 'price-asc' | 'price-desc' | 'name-asc'. FilterState interface if multiple filters are grouped.

questions.question: Parent re-renders but ProductCard has React.memo — cards still all re-render. Why?
questions.sampleAnswer: memo does shallow compare. If I pass onAdd={() => add(product.id)} or style={{ width: 100 }} every render, props are new references — memo bails out of skipping. Fix: stable callbacks with useCallback, pass primitives, or pass product from memoized list with stable references.

questions.question: A product list of 500 items lags when typing in search. What do you investigate?
keyPoints: Re-filtering on every keystroke without useMemo
questions.sampleAnswer: I profile with React DevTools. Check if filtered list recomputes every render — wrap in useMemo. Debounce search input like Day 09. Verify ProductCard memo and stable props. For 500+ DOM nodes, mention virtualization (react-window) as next step — not required today.

questions.question: Teammate mutates products array with products.sort() in render. What's wrong?
keyPoints: Same reference — React may skip re-render
questions.sampleAnswer: sort mutates in place. If products is state, they mutated state directly — React may not detect change (same reference) or cause inconsistent UI. Fix: const sorted = [...products].sort(...). Set state with new array. Same immutability rule applies to cart on Day 12.
redFlags: Mutation is fine if it works
